goal: Identity gate on every write path; surface missing-identity in status/context

summary.title: Identity gate on every write path; surface missing-identity in status/context
summary.what: Fixes a design-level invariant violation: a fresh clone of a mainline-enabled repo could run write commands (start, append, seal, abandon, pin, merge, publish, check submit) without a local actor identity. requireInit only checked .mainline/config.toml (committed); .ml-cache/identity.json (per-actor, gitignored) was absent on a peer clone. New requireIdentity() gate is wired into every write path. SealSubmit specifically reordered to do identity + cfg lookup BEFORE any draft mutation — pre-fix it would mutate draft to sealed_local then fail the identity check, leaving the draft unrecoverable. StatusResult and ContextResult gain identity_configured boolean; CLI status warns when missing.
summary.why: Bug filed by an alpha tester (well-formed report with reproduction). The corruption path was real: a draft going from drafting -> sealed_local with no actor-log event written meant the user could not retry seal even after running init, and the only recovery was to manually edit the JSON file in .ml-cache/drafts/. Worse, fresh-clone is the canonical onboarding step for the friend MVP rollout — the very first interaction would have been broken in a way that erodes trust.
summary.user_goal: Make a fresh clone fail loudly and recoverably when identity is missing, instead of silently creating malformed drafts and corrupting state on first write.

decisions.point: requireIdentity returns the identity (used by callers) vs returns only error
decisions.rationale: Most write callsites need the identity right after gating. Returning it from requireIdentity collapses the two-call pattern (requireIdentity then getIdentity) and removes a class of mistakes where a caller called only one of them.

decisions.point: Reorder SealSubmit vs leave the draft mutation where it was and just gate earlier
decisions.chose: reorder: identity + cfg lookup at top, mutation at bottom
decisions.rationale: The all-validation-before-mutation discipline is what kept the v0.3 snapshot contract clean. Same principle applies to identity. The reorder is a one-line change but it makes the function a textbook example of the pattern: every gate runs before any state changes.

decisions.point: Empty ActorID is treated identically to missing identity file
decisions.chose: reject both with the same recoverable error
decisions.rationale: Either failure mode means the same thing to the user — `mainline init --actor-name` fixes it. Differentiating in the error would just be noise; the recoverable suggestion is identical.

decisions.chose: add as a new boolean field
decisions.rationale: Inferring "is identity configured" from "is actor_id non-empty" works for now, but having an explicit field lets future code distinguish "no actor_id because not configured" from "no actor_id because the actor is unknown for some other reason" without ambiguity.

decisions.point: CLI warning placement — top of status output vs inline
decisions.rationale: Missing identity is the highest-priority piece of information when it applies; pushing it down below "Branch:" / "Actor: (missing)" / etc. would let users skim past it. A single bold warning at the top is the right hierarchy.

rejected.alternative: Fix only SealSubmit (the corruption path) and leave start/append permissive
rejected.reason: Would still let users create drafts with actor_id=""; those drafts later cause cascading sync issues (events in nobody actor log, unable to publish). Need to gate at the source.

rejected.alternative: Auto-create an identity on first write if the user has git user.name set
rejected.reason: Hides the requirement and makes the actor-id non-deterministic. Better to fail loud and recoverable than to surprise users with a silently-generated identity that propagates to other clones.
Works today but conflates "missing identity" with "actor_id was deleted from a valid identity file" (a corruption case). Explicit field is clearer.

rejected.alternative: Block status/context commands when identity is missing
rejected.reason: Read commands should always work; they are how the user discovers the missing-identity state. The warning is enough.

risks: Tests in this package use Init via testRepo+svc.Init which writes identity. Any future test that wipes identity would now fail at requireIdentity rather than continue with empty actor_id; this surfaced via the new identity_gate_test setup, all existing tests still pass.

Auto-pin runs inside Sync. Sync itself is read-only at the gate level (no requireIdentity). But auto-pin (Service.Pin) is now gated. So a sync from a fresh clone will rebuild the view but skip auto-pin with an error. The error currently propagates as a sync warning; ideal would be to surface "identity missing — auto-pin disabled" specifically. Documented as followup.
External callers of Service.Pin / SealSubmit would need to handle the new error class, but there are no external callers — these are internal-package methods.
followups: Sync should surface "identity missing — auto-pin disabled" as a structured warning rather than silently returning the gate error from Pin. Current behaviour: sync continues; user sees no auto-pin output but also no clear reason.

Consider gating Sync itself when identity is missing. Pro: prevents creating .ml-cache/views/ with stale data attributed to no actor. Con: blocks the discovery path (running sync to see "what is here"). Opted not to gate sync now; revisit if it bites.
doctor --setup --fix could detect missing-identity and prompt for actor-name as part of the fix-up flow. Currently doctor checks refspecs and AGENTS.md; identity belongs in that family.
Add a CLI hint to "mainline init" when identity already exists: "identity already configured for actor X, use --rewire to reset" — currently it silently re-creates.

architectural_claims: Every write path on Service requires a non-empty local actor identity before any state mutation; the gate is requireIdentity() and it is enforced at the entry of each method.
SealSubmitWithOptions runs all validation gates (identity, JSON parse, draft state, snapshot contract) before any draft mutation — failure modes leave the draft in its original state, retryable.
Read surfaces (StatusResult, ContextResult) carry an explicit identity_configured boolean alongside the conditional ActorID field.
behavioral_changes: Fresh clone (no .ml-cache/identity.json) returns a recoverable error from start/append/seal/abandon/pin/merge/publish/check submit, pointing at `mainline init --actor-name`. Was: silently created malformed drafts.
Failed SealSubmit due to missing identity now leaves the draft in drafting status. Was: corrupted draft to sealed_local with no actor-log event.
mainline status prints a one-line warning at the top when identity_configured=false.

api_changes.signature: mainline status now warns when identity is missing
